subject: mt7601u possible fix?
author: bbond007

upstream.vanilla_quote: Line 592: mt7601u_mcu_calibrate(dev, MCU_CAL_DPD, dev->curr_temp); Lines 1159-1167: ret = mt7601u_mcu_calibrate(dev, MCU_CAL_TXIQ, 0); ... ret = mt7601u_mcu_calibrate(dev, MCU_CAL_RXIQ, 0); ... ret = mt7601u_mcu_calibrate(dev, MCU_CAL_DPD, dev->dpd_temp);

impact.effect_if_absent: Device performs full PHY calibration at initialization and post-association (stock behavior). Applying this patch would disable MCU_CAL_DPD and MCU_CAL_RXIQ calibrations, potentially degrading wireless stability or performance.

notes: Unprincipled experimental/debugging change with vague commit message ('mt7601u possible fix?') and stray test markers in the diff ('remove this for testing--> spark2k06'). The commit comments out three PHY calibration MCU calls: (1) MCU_CAL_DPD in mt7601u_phy_recalibrate_after_assoc (line 592), and (2) MCU_CAL_TXIQ, MCU_CAL_RXIQ, MCU_CAL_DPD in mt7601u_init_cal (lines 1159-1167). Vanilla 6.18.38 retains all these calibrations enabled, contradicting the patch's intent. Not carried in buildroot linux-patches/. Patch-provenance.md F-4 explicitly addresses this as a deliberate drop decision, assessing the change as 'unprincipled' and 'not in P1.9's task scope'. Re-add only if a documented mt7601u regression appears at P3.13 with hardware verification. CONFIG_MT7601U=m is enabled in our kernel config; firmware BR2_PACKAGE_LINUX_FIRMWARE_MEDIATEK_MT7601U=y is included.
